zh.Arwennack, Cornwall, England, United Kin: 英國英格蘭可瓦爾阿爾文納可
zh.of Ardevora, Philleigh, Cornwall, Englan: 英國英格蘭可瓦爾費爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Jane Petit於大約1453年出生在英國可瓦爾費爾時，她的父親Valentine Pettit25歲，母親Jane Anthorne24歲。她於大約1483年與John Killigrew在英國可瓦爾費爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1518年在英國可瓦爾福維享年66歲，埋葬在英國可瓦爾福維。

zh-hans.of Ardevora, Philleigh, Cornwall, Englan: 英国英格兰可瓦爾費爾
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jane Petit于大约1453年出生在英国可瓦爾費爾时，她的父亲Valentine Pettit25岁，母亲Jane Anthorne24岁。她于大约1483年与John Killigrew在英国可瓦爾費爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1518年在英国可瓦爾福維享年66岁，埋葬在英国可瓦爾福維。

nameMeanings.name: Petit
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French/Haitian Alain, Michel, Normand, Mireille, Philippe, Pierre, Achille, Armand, Jean Claude, Jean Jacques, Ketly, Adrien.

French, Walloon, Catalan, English, Jewish (from southern France), West Indian (mainly Haiti), and Mauritian: from (Old) French, Catalan, and Middle English petit ‘small’, hence a nickname for a small man (or an ironic nickname for a big man), or a distinguishing name for the younger of two bearers of the same personal name. Compare Little , Petee , and Petite .

History: Charles Petit from Anneville-Ambourville in Seine-Maritime, France, married Jeanne Rossignol (Martin and Renée Desjardins) in Quebec City, QC, in 1670. Nicolas Petit dit Lapré/Laprée from Le Gué-d'Alleré in Charente-Maritime, France, married Marie Pouponnel in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1656. Pierre Petit dit Gobin from Lyon in Rhône married Marguerite Véron in Trois-Rivières, QC, in 1692. Pierre Petit dit Saint-Pierre from Évreux in Eure, France, married Judith Miville in Quebec City, QC, in 1758; see also St. Pierre . — This surname is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of America and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of South Carolina.

variants.name: Pattee
meanings: Altered form of French (Nord) Pety: variant of Petit . This surname was brought to North America from England.

variants.name: Petion
meanings: French and Haitian (Pétion): diminutive of Petit . This surname was brought to the US from Haiti, where it is by far most common.

variants.name: Petite
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Celestine, Jacques.
French: from the feminine form of   Petit   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

variants.name: Petitt
meanings: English: variant of   Petit  .

variants.name: Pettett
meanings: English: variant of   Petit  .

variants.name: Little
meanings: English: nickname for a small man (if not ironic), or distinguishing epithet for the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, from Middle English littel, Old English lȳtel (see Light 3).
Irish: translation into English of Gaelic Ó Beagáin ‘descendant of Beagán’ (see Began ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of any of various European surnames meaning ‘little’, e.g. French Petit , Polish Mały (see Maly 1).

variants.name: Petee
meanings: Americanized form of French   Petit  .

variants.name: Pettie
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Petit  .

variants.name: Pettit
meanings: English (mainly southeastern): variant of   Petit  .

variants.name: Petito
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Angelo, Carmine, Pasquale, Antonio, Rocco, Alfonzo, Alphonso, Antimo, Emilio, Giovanni, Giuseppe, Orazio, Salvatore, Silvio, Tullio, Virginio.
Italian: nickname for a small man (or an ironic nickname for a tall one), from dialect petito ‘small’ or from the personal name Petito; compare French Petit .

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jaine
meanings: Variant spelling of   Jane .

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Jane Petit was born about 1453, in Philleigh, Cornwall, England, her father, Valentine Pettit, was 25 and her mother, Jane Anthorne, was 24. She married John Killigrew about 1483, in Philleigh, Cornwall, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1518, in Fowey, Cornwall, England, at the age of 66, and was buried in Fowey, Cornwall, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jane Petit est née environ 1453, à Philleigh, Cornouailles, Angleterre, son père, Valentine Pettit, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Jane Anthorne, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé John Killigrew environ 1483, à Philleigh, Cornouailles, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1518, à Fowey, Cornouailles, Angleterre, à l’âge de 66 ans, et a été enterrée à Fowey, Cornouailles, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jane Petit um 1453 in Philleigh, Cornwall, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Valentine Pettit 25 und ihre Mutter Jane Anthorne 24. Sie hat um 1483 in Philleigh, Cornwall, England John Killigrew geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1518 im Alter von 66 Jahren in Fowey, Cornwall, England und wurde in Fowey, Cornwall, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Petit è nata il circa 1453 nel Philleigh, Cornwall, England, sua padre, Valentine Pettit, aveva 25 anni e sua madre, Jane Anthorne, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato John Killigrew circa 1483, nel Philleigh, Cornwall, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1518, nel Fowey, Cornwall, England, all'età di 66 anni, e fu sepolta a Fowey, Cornwall, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Jane Petitが約1453年にイギリスコルンワルフィレイグで生まれた時，父Valentine Pettitは25歳，母Jane Anthorneは24歳であった。約1483年にイギリスコルンワルフィレイグでJohn Killigrewと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1518年にイギリスコルンワルフォウエイで66歳で死去し，イギリスコルンワルフォウエイにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Jane Petit(이)가 대략 1453년 영국 코른월 필리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Valentine Pettit(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jane Anthorne(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 대략 1483년 영국 코른월 필리에서 John Killigrew(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1518년 영국 코른월 파우이에서 66세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 코른월 파우이에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jane Petit nasceu aproximadamente 1453, em Philleigh, Cornwall, Inglaterra, seu pai, Valentine Pettit, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Jane Anthorne, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com John Killigrew aproximadamente 1483, em Philleigh, Cornwall, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 1518, em Fowey, Cornwall, Inglaterra, com 66 anos, e foi sepultada em Fowey, Cornwall, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jane Petit родилась около 1453 г. в местности филлиг, корнвалл, Англия, её отцу по имени Valentine Pettit было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Jane Anthorne, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Killigrew около 1483 г. в местности филлиг, корнвалл, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1518 г. в местности фоуи, корнвалл, Англия в возрасте 66 лет и была похоронена в местности фоуи, корнвалл, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jane Petit nació aproximadamente 1453, en Philleigh, Cornwall, Inglaterra, su papá, Valentine Pettit, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Jane Anthorne, tenía 24 años. Se casó con John Killigrew aproximadamente 1483, en Philleigh, Cornwall, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1518, en Fowey, Cornwall, Inglaterra, a la edad de 66 años, y fue sepultada en Fowey, Cornwall, Inglaterra.
